---
title: "Module 6–7: Output Formats & Capstone"
subtitle: "HTML, Word, PDF, Themes — Then Build a Complete Report"
author: "NIEHS Training"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  ioslides_presentation:
    widescreen: true
    css: rmd_slides_style.css
    self_contained: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, comment = "")
```

# Output Formats {.segue}

## The Big Three

| Format | YAML | Best for |
|--------|------|----------|
| **HTML** | `html_document` | Interactive, sharing online |
| **Word** | `word_document` | Manuscripts, institutional reports |
| **PDF** | `pdf_document` | Formal publications (needs LaTeX) |

> We use HTML for this workshop. It's fastest, needs no extra setup, and
> supports interactive features.

## HTML Themes

Change `theme:` in the YAML to instantly restyle:

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
```

Built-in themes: `default`, `bootstrap`, `cerulean`, `cosmo`, `darkly`,
`flatly`, `journal`, `lumen`, `paper`, `readable`, `sandstone`, `simplex`,
`spacelab`, `united`, `yeti`

> **Exercise:** Change `theme: flatly` to `theme: journal`, re-knit, see the difference.

## Syntax Highlighting

Control code coloring:

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    highlight: tango
```

Styles: `default`, `tango`, `pygments`, `kate`, `monochrome`, `espresso`,
`zenburn`, `haddock`, `breezedark`

## Table of Contents

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true            # Enable TOC
    toc_float: true      # Floating sidebar TOC
    toc_depth: 3         # Include H1–H3
    number_sections: true
```

The floating TOC you've seen in every notebook today is configured this way.

## Code Folding

Let readers show/hide code on demand:

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide    # Hidden by default, click to show
    # or: show            # Shown by default, click to hide
```

> Perfect for reports shared with non-coders who want results but may want
> to peek at the code.

## Data Frame Printing

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: kable     # Auto-style data frames as tables
    # or: paged          # Paginated HTML tables
    # or: default        # Plain text
```

## Word Output

```yaml
output: word_document
```

### With a template:

```yaml
output:
  word_document:
    reference_docx: "templates/niehs_template.docx"
```

Create a template: Knit a basic Rmd to Word → modify styles → save as template.

**Limitations:** No interactive tables, no plotly, no code folding, no floating TOC.

## PDF Output

```yaml
output: pdf_document
```

Requires LaTeX. On Posit Cloud:

```r
install.packages("tinytex")
tinytex::install_tinytex()  # Takes several minutes
```

> Stick with HTML for this workshop. Set up PDF later if needed.

## Multiple Formats in One YAML

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
  word_document:
    reference_docx: "template.docx"
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
```

The **Knit dropdown** lets you choose which format.

## Polished YAML — Full Example

```yaml
---
title: "PFAS Dose-Response Analysis"
subtitle: "Supplementary Methods"
author:
  - "Jane Smith, Ph.D. — Biostatistics, NIEHS"
  - "John Doe, Ph.D. — Epidemiology, NIEHS"
date: "`r knitr::inline_expr('format(Sys.Date(), \"%B %d, %Y\")')`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: kable
params:
  data_file: "data/pfas_2024.csv"
---
```

## Custom CSS

For full control over appearance:

```yaml
output:
  html_document:
    css: custom_styles.css
```

Or embed CSS directly in the `.Rmd`:

````
```{css, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
.title { color: #1a365d; }
blockquote {
  border-left: 4px solid #3182CE;
  background-color: #EBF8FF;
}
```
````

# Capstone Exercise {.segue}

## Build a Complete Analysis Report | 20 min

Open **`07_capstone_exercise.Rmd`** and fill in the sections:

1. **YAML:** Change author to your name
2. **Introduction:** 2–3 sentences with **bold** and *italic*
3. **Methods:** Describe the model; write the equation with `$$`
4. **Descriptive table:** `group_by() %>% summarise()` → `kable()`
5. **Tabbed section:** Histogram + Boxplot in separate tabs
6. **Regression:** `lm()` → `broom::tidy()` → `kable()`
7. **Figure:** Scatterplot with `geom_smooth()` and `fig.cap`
8. **Discussion:** Interpret results using **inline code**

> The data is pre-generated in the notebook. You just fill in the analysis.

## Skills You'll Practice

| Skill | Where |
|-------|-------|
| YAML configuration | Header |
| Markdown formatting | Introduction, Discussion |
| Code chunks with options | Throughout |
| Inline code | Methods, Discussion |
| `kable()` + `kableExtra` | Descriptive table, Regression |
| `ggplot2` + figure options | Histogram, Boxplot, Scatterplot |
| LaTeX equations | Methods section |
| Tabbed sections | Exposure Distribution |
| `code_folding: hide` | YAML setting |

# You're an R Markdown Author Now! {.segue}

## Today You Learned

✅ Create R Markdown documents with YAML, Markdown, and code chunks

✅ Control output with chunk options (`echo`, `eval`, `include`)

✅ Report statistics dynamically with inline code

✅ Build publication-quality tables with `kable()` and `kableExtra`

✅ Create and size figures with `ggplot2`, `patchwork`, and `plotly`

✅ Write equations, use parameters, and add citations

✅ Produce HTML, Word, and PDF output with themes

## Resources

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| R Markdown: The Definitive Guide | bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown |
| R Markdown Cookbook | bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown-cookbook |
| R Markdown Cheat Sheet | rstudio.com/resources/cheatsheets |
| Posit Cloud Docs | docs.posit.co/cloud |
| kableExtra vignettes | haozhu233.github.io/kableExtra |

## Quick Reference

| Action | Shortcut |
|--------|----------|
| Knit document | `Ctrl+Shift+K` |
| Insert chunk | `Ctrl+Alt+I` |
| Run chunk | `Ctrl+Shift+Enter` |
| Run line | `Ctrl+Enter` |
| Inline code | `` `r knitr::inline_expr('expr')` `` |
| Bold | `**text**` |
| Italic | `*text*` |
| Display equation | `$$..$$` |
